The story is longer and more confusing and complex, but thatâs the short version. ð
One has killed themselves. The father has died. I drove my mum to the funeral because she wanted to make sure he was buried.

She only found out I was adopted after an argument with her parents after finding out she was pregnant again... this time to an actual boyfriend(instead of her parents and brothers), yes her brothers and father, are my father. Which one... no idea.
She spent years looking for me assuming that I was dead, but was actually alive.
When I was both she was told that she gave birth to twins, but we both died.
She put me up for adoption because she was just on 14 when she got pregnant, 15 when she had me. So there isnât a lot of age difference between us.
She was abused like a motherfucker all her life.
My mother grew up here in Australia. Her mother on the NZ side married a bloke with a lot of Japanese and European in him, so Iâm part Maori/Japanese basically.

The story gets so involved itâs not even funny. But my great great grandfather is Hone Heke, the instigator of the flagstaff wars in NZ, the first Maori chief to sign the Waitangi Treaty. Iâm basically NZ royalty.
So we started off with letters, and now weâre best friends.
The register got back to me and said that she was interested in getting in touch as well.
Decided to go on the register to look for my real mother around 18 years of age. Thatâs the legal minimum here to find your parents.
Grew up with two younger brothers and sisters with my adopted parents. Both of them were too handicapped to really relate to or anything, but theyâre funny guys, generally. (And girl).
Always knew I was adopted, Mum and Dad told me since day one. I canât remember the first time I was told, but they told me enough that I knew since I can remember.
Spent about 4 months of the start of my life in a humidicrib.
I was adopted when I was about 6 months old I think.
Thought Iâd better ask first, do you actually wanna hear this?
I wasnât going to just blurt it out like, oh here Iâm forcing this story on you. lol
So Iâve got this epic story hey.
Iâll tell you a story one day about adoption.
I'm a social worker that helps foster kids get adopted and a mom to a 8 yr old girl
What do you do when youâre not posting memes and being awesome?
Iâve got epilepsy from your legs
